name: Recent Edits

releases.author: cwagner223355
releases.description:
Plugin-review fixes for community-store submission.

- Bump `minAppVersion` to `1.7.2` (required for `Workspace.revealLeaf`)
- Use `Vault.configDir` instead of hardcoded `.obsidian` reference
- Use `FileManager.trashFile()` instead of `Vault.trash()` (respects user deletion preference)
- Replace `builtin-modules` package with native `node:module`
- Shorter command id and name (no plugin id/name duplication)
- Drop "General" settings heading
- Wrap unawaited promises in `void` and fix async-callback misuse warnings

releases.author: cwagner223355
releases.description: Prepare for relate to Obsidian Community Plugin Directory

releases.author: cwagner223355
releases.description:
## Bug fix

Fixes a regression introduced in v1.2.0 where external edits (Claude Code, scripts, other filesystem writes) on a file that had been previously edited in Obsidian would show the stale Obsidian edit time instead of the new external edit time.

### What changed

The v1.2.0 cross-device mtime persistence used an "obsidian-only-write" rule to keep mobile sync deliveries from clobbering canonical timestamps. That rule was too broad — it also blocked legitimate local external edits on desktop from updating the canonical mtime.

v1.2.1 splits the rule by platform:

- **Obsidian edits** still always update the canonical (unchanged).
- **Desktop external edits** now update the canonical, with a two-signal guard against the rare case of a sync delivery arriving from mobile: write only if the mtime advance is greater than 60s OR if the file had a recent editor-change on this device (within 5 min).
- **Mobile external edits** still never update the canonical, preserving the v1.2.0 cross-device fix.

releases.author: cwagner223355
releases.description:
## What's new in v1.2.0

### Cross-device mtime persistence
Files synced via Obsidian Sync now appear in the correct day groups on mobile. The plugin persists a canonical `_editTimes` map written only on the originating device, so mobile's sync-receipt timestamps don't jumble the panel order. Requires "Sync settings" to be enabled in your Obsidian Sync config so `data.json` travels with the vault.

### Copy absolute path affordance setting
New setting: **Copy absolute path affordance** (`button` / `path-text` / `both`, default `button`). In v1.1.0 the folder-path text was always a click target, which was easy to trigger accidentally. The new default puts an explicit link icon button above the time instead. Users who prefer the subtler path-text behavior can switch from settings.

releases.author: cwagner223355
releases.description: Added the ability to get full path by clicking on line 2.

releases.author: cwagner223355
releases.description: ## Recent Edits A sidebar panel that shows files modified in the last *N* days, grouped by calendar day. Marks files edited from outside Obsidian — filesystem writes, sync, plugins — with a configurable indicator dot. ![Recent Edits panel](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/cwagner223355/obsidian-recent-edits/main/assets/Recent%20Edits%20Screenshot.png) ### Why? Most **Recent Files** plugins for Obsidian show recently *opened* files, not recently *modified* ones. As I use my vault together with AI tools, I thought it would be useful to see recently modified files and distinguish between edits I've made in Obsidian and those made by the file system. Useful when: - An external editor or script writes through the filesystem - AI assistants edit notes via filesystem APIs Recent Edits closes that gap. It shows what changed, when it changed, and visually flags edits that came from outside Obsidian's editor. ### Highlights - Files modified in the last 7 days (configurable, 1–90 days), grouped by calendar day - Day headers labelled `Today` / `Yesterday` / `YYYY-MM-DD (Ddd)`, sorted most recent first - Configurable indicator dot for edits that came from outside Obsidian's editor - "Background folders" toggle: hide noisy folders by default, reveal inline via toggle - "Excluded folders" to permanently hide certain edits - Optional hover preview (uses the Page Preview core plugin) - Includes `.md`, `.canvas`, and `.base` files ### Install (manual until accepted into the community store) 1. Download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` from this release. 2. Place them in `/.obsidian/plugins/recent-edits/`. 3. In Obsidian: Settings → Community plugins → Refresh, then enable **Recent Edits**. 4. Click the clock/history ribbon icon, or run **Open Recent Edits panel** from the command palette. ### Potential Limitations The external-edit status is meant to be an indication. I've tried to make it accurate for my setup, but variations in setup or changes in the future mean you should treat it as an indicator rather than a perfect signal. It could mistake: - Edits arriving via Obsidian Sync from another device. - Writes from other plugins (Templater, Daily Notes, etc.). - Files modified before the plugin was installed (these stay unclassified until the next time they're touched). ### Support If Recent Edits is useful to you, consider buying me a coffee:
releases.name: 1.0.0 — Initial release
